data.title: Home price growth cools more than expected in March : S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Home Price
data.description:
February S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Home Price Index for 20 cities slipped -0.1% M/M vs. +0.2% prior, according to data released on Tuesday.

On an unadjusted basis, the 20-city index rose +0.4% M/M vs. -0.1% prior. That resulted in a +0.9% Y/Y increase vs. 1.1% consensus and +1.2% in January.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Consumer IPO scorecard: Early gains for Yesway; slumps for BRCB, BOBS, and OFRM
data.description:
Black Rock Coffee Bar (BRCB) priced its IPO on September 11, 2025, at $20 per share, above the top of its $16 to $18 range. The post-IPO range for the stock is $11.51 to $30.40. Shares were trading below $12 at last check. Black Rock Coffee Bar is a drive-thru and sit-in coffee chain operating across several western U.S. states. The company serves caffeinated beverages and other coffee shop items through its café network. 

Bob's Discount Furniture (BOBS) priced its IPO on February 4 at $17 per share, the bottom of its $17 to $19 range. The stock has slumped since the trading debut and currently is swapping hands at $11.72. Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) is a home furnishings retailer that sells furniture and mattresses through showrooms and an omnichannel retail model. It was founded in 1991 and has grown into a nationally known value-oriented furniture chain.

Once Upon A Farm (OFRM) priced its IPO on February 5 at $18 per share, within its $17 to $19 range. After a strong first couple of days of trading that took it all the way up to $27.00, the stock has seen a steady decline and is currently swapping hands below $15. Once Upon a Farm (OFRM) makes organic children’s food products, including pouches, frozen meals, oat bars, and snacks. The company focuses on organic, non-GMO offerings without added sugar or artificial ingredients.

Yesway (YSWY) priced its IPO on April 21 at $20 per share, the low end of its $20 to $23 range. So far, Yesway (YSWY) is attracting positive attention and reached a post-IPO high of $22.80 on Monday. Yesway (YSWY) is a convenience store operator with stores under the Yesway and Allsup’s brands. Its business is centered on fuel, grocery items, food service, and private-label products, including Allsup’s burritos.

data.title: Starbucks earnings preview: China, tariffs, and loyalty in focus
data.description:
Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) are drawing attention on Tuesday as investors position ahead of the company’s upcoming earnings release, scheduled after the closing bell. The stock is trading modestly higher in pre-market trading, up about 0.4% and hovering around the $98 level.

Market participants are increasingly looking to prediction platform Kalshi for insight into sentiment surrounding the report. Traders are actively pricing in expectations for key themes likely to emerge during management’s commentary.

Among the primary areas of focus are potential impacts from China-related tariffs and updates to Starbucks’ loyalty program performance. Together, these factors are expected to shape investor reaction and provide clues about the company’s near-term growth trajectory. 

Outlined below is what traders on Kalshi are forecasting SBUX to say in its upcoming earnings call: 

  • China — 98% chance. 
  • Protein — 96% chance.
  • Boyu — 87% chance. 
  • Tariff — 85% chance.
  • Smart Queue / SmartQ — 77% chance. 
  • Holiday — 69% chance. 
  • Loyalty — 69% chance. 
  • Canada — 44% chance.
  • Promotion — 41% chance. 
  • Recycle / Recyclable — 25% chance. 
  • Non Dairy — 22% chance. 
  • Comparable Sales — 20% chance. 
  • Condiment Bar — 18% chance.
  • Oatmilk / Oat Milk — 17% chance. 
  • Union — 13% chance.
  • Peppermint — 9% chance.

data.title: Semis flash dot-com-speed momentum
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks (SMH) have been rallying at a pace not seen since the internet bubble’s most violent upside bursts, raising questions about market sustainability.

Turning Point Market Research’s Dean Christians noted that during the peak 1990s run in internet services, there were 15 instances when the sector’s 18-day rate of change exceeded 36%.

Semiconductors are now approaching that same threshold, TPMR noted, a move largely driven by artificial intelligence (AIQ) capital expenditure and data-center demand.

The pattern does not automatically signal an end to the rally. The dot-com era featured many massive surges before the final top; however, it does demonstrate how compressed the semiconductor move has become.

data.title: The Polaris International Equity Composite adds RYAAY; exits CAPMF, MEOH
data.description:
• The Polaris International Equity Composite delivered a 5.13% net return in Q1 2026, compared with a 1.12% decline in the MSCI EAFE Index, marking clear outperformance.
  • The fund initiated a new position in Ryanair Holdings (NASDAQ: RYAAY) in Q12026.
  • The portfolio fully exited Capgemini SE (CAPMF) and Methanex (NASDAQ: MEOH).
  • Source.

data.title: Amkor tumbles as Arizona ramp-up leads to short-term margin decline; analysts remain upbeat
data.description:
Amkor Technology (AMKR) plunged 13% by early trading on Tuesday after reporting first-quarter results and guidance that surpassed consensus estimates.

However, management said the development of its campus in Arizona will likely lead to a short-term dilution of its operating income margin before it enters production in 2028.

"Similar to our Vietnam ramp-up phase, these preparation costs will be recognized in OpEx until programs are qualified for production, at which point they will transition to cost of goods sold," said Amkor CFO Megan Faust during the earnings call on Monday evening. "As a result, we anticipate this will start to dilute operating income margin by approximately 1% to 2%, beginning in 2027 and improving in 2028. Once at full scale, we expect Arizona will be a significant driver of operating income margin expansion, reflecting the benefits of high-value advanced packaging at what is planned to be our most automated factory."

Financial firms were positive on the company's results and guidance.

Needham maintained its Buy rating and increased its price target on the stock to $90 from $65.

"For 2026, Computing is still expected up >20%, with AI/DC-related strength offsetting weakness in PC. Automotive/Industrial is likely the second strongest segment, and Communications is expected up high-single to low double digits (thanks to exposure to iOS), whereas Consumer is likely to be a laggard and flat to up low single digits," said Needham analysts Charles Shi and Denis Pyatchanin in a Tuesday note. "On the flip side, management expects OM dilution due to Arizona ramp-up costs flowing through OpEx next year."

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley retained its Equal-weight rating and increased its price target to $69 from $45.

"Amkor is executing toward its mid- to high-teens GM% target by 2H, where we now model for 17.5%/18.5% exiting FY26/FY27, driving our 2027 EPS revision from $1.95 to $2.30," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, in a note. "In the current supply/demand environment, management called out a more favorable pricing environment, capacity flexibility across HDFO/2.5D, and broad-based demand—all of which should support 2H guidance."

Amkor competitor ASE Technology (ASX) was down nearly 3% during early Tuesday trading.

data.title: Rambus falls after rating cut at Baird
data.description:
Shares of Rambus (RMBS) fell about 18% premarket on Tuesday following first quarter results and a rating cut by Baird Equity.

The firm downgraded Rambus — which offers memory interface chips — to Neutral from Outperform while keeping the price target unchanged at $120.

"While the acceleration in x86 demand, notably driven by agentic AI, is positive for Rambus, we are increasingly seeing the potential for a slowdown in RDIMM YoY growth next year due to increasing DRAM shortages which we expect will persist throughout 2027 (with most of the new DRAM capacity going to HBM), while rocketing DRAM pricing may impact MRDIMM volumes as well. Rambus is the classic case of a unit-driven top-line impacted at times of severe memory shortages without the benefit of higher pricing," said analysts led by Tristan Gerra.

The analysts noted that Rambus remains one of the highest-quality names within their small-cap coverage list.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Tuesday: LLY, UPS, RITM, SPGI
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed before the bell as investors awaited more earnings while U.S.-Iran updates remained in the forefront.

Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

S&P Global (SPGI) was trading 1.01% higher in premarket trade after first-quarter earnings exceeded analyst estimates. The company, known for its S&P 500 index and credit ratings, reported first-quarter adjusted diluted earnings per share of $4.97, up 14% from the prior-year quarter and exceeding the consensus of $4.82. The full-year 2026 revenue guidance was revised lower.

Eli Lilly (LLY) was up 0.38% before the opening bell following news of a research collaboration with Profluent, a biotech firm focused on AI-driven drug discovery. The deal, worth up to $2.25 billion, aims to develop and commercialize genetic medicines. As part of the agreement, Profluent will leverage its AI technologies to design and optimize recombinases, a group of enzymes commonly used in gene editing.

Rithm Capital (RITM) was trading 1.48% higher at $10.27 during premarket trading after the mortgage REIT’s Q1 earnings exceeded analyst expectations. The New York-based company posted Q1 non-GAAP EPS of $0.51, which exceeds the S&P Cap IQ consensus estimate of $0.50 but lags the prior-quarter figure of $0.74. The company recorded growth in mortgage origination and servicing, residential lending, and asset management.

United Parcel Service (UPS) was down 4.54% in premarket trade. The company holds the most attractive valuation among major air freight and logistics stocks, earning an A- valuation grade in Seeking Alpha’s quantitative rankings. The grade measures how a stock is priced relative to its fundamentals, with UPS’s standing reflecting how competitively priced the stock remains relative to its underlying business fundamentals.

data.title: Fuel Tech secures $10M in air pollution control contracts
data.description:
• Fuel Tech (FTEK) announced on Tuesday that it has been awarded multiple air pollution control (APC) contracts valued at approximately $10M with domestic utility and industrial customers.
  • The awards include a contract to integrate Fuel Tech’s selective catalytic reduction (SCR) technology with two new natural gas-fired turbines for a publicly owned Midwest municipal utility, as well as two orders from existing industrial customers for SNCR and SCR upgrades. 
  • The largest award calls for integration of Fuel Tech’s SCR pollution control systems with two new gas turbines that will increase the municipal utility’s plant output by approximately 100 MW.
  • The expanded generating station is expected to become operational in 2029. Fuel Tech indicated it will commence engineering work immediately and that equipment deliveries are scheduled to begin in late 2027.

data.title: Apple's Q2 results may offer 'slight' upside, guidance may be a positive surprise: UBS
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is set to report fiscal second-quarter results on April 30, and investment firm UBS is expecting a “slight” upside to the consensus, based on Mac and iPhone-related strength.

“Supply chain checks and analysis of sell-through data suggest modest upside to our iPhone ests while demand for the Neo and Mac mini suggests demand at least in-line with our Mac rev forecast,” UBS analyst David Vogt wrote in a note to clients. Vogt reiterated his Neutral rating on Apple but raised his price target to $287 from $280.

Apple shares rose fractionally in premarket trading on Tuesday, bucking the broader tech sell-off.

Turning to guidance, Vogt said he is expecting a “solid” offering for the June quarter and upped his earnings estimates as a result.

“Rising memory prices and Apple's ability to secure adequate supply is driving smartphone share gains for the iPhone product line at the expense of the broader Android ecosystem in our view,” Vogt explained. “In addition, solid demand for the Mac portfolio in part driven by initial success of the lower priced 'Neo' as well as demand for the 'mini' increasingly used to run AI agents like OpenClaw should drive incremental upside relative to our expectations.”

A consensus of analysts expects Apple to earn $1.94 per share on $109.73B in revenue for the fiscal second quarter.
